meta.setting: Redding road yards, Shasta wagon stops, French Gulch approaches, and Pit River/Wintu/Yana homelands
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with labor pressure, road knowledge, kin networks, land rights, prejudice, and hard choices
meta.quality_bar: hand-cut, grounded, respectful, table-useful, western-only language

research_grounding.title: Redding Rancheria
research_grounding.use: Federally recognized Pit River, Wintu, and Yana people, sovereignty, culture, identity, and traditional homelands.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek Wintu context, displacement pressure, allotments, and perseverance.

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Regional gold rush context, roads, immigrant communities, and settlement pressure.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Pit River Hands cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented respectful work-yard note or road observation generated later; do not use fake Native language
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Ridge and River Workers
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing Pit River, Wintu, and Yana-connected workers, riders, and families navigating labor, land, and road pressure
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four grounded signs, strengths, or social facts
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate this as a grounded labor-and-community presence tied to the broader Shasta and Redding region.
Tie future moves to kin networks, local work, route knowledge, refusal, public prejudice, quiet leverage, wagon-yard gossip, and knowing which official paper will fail first.
Make the page useful for harness repair, freight sorting, road negotiation, family protection, witness scenes, and local accountability.
Do not invent sacred details, fake words, decorative costumes, or special powers.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: Let the page carry the pressure of people being asked for knowledge by the same county that ignores their claims.
generation_prompt.table_spark: The person paid to mend your harness may know which road your warrant will take.
must_preserve: Use respectful 1899 western and regional language only.
Do not invent sacred practices, fake words, or mystical traits.
Represent Native people as living families, workers, stewards, negotiators, and political actors.
The generated page must include image path cast/images/pit-river-hands.jpg.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred-invented, or fake-tribal framing
do not reduce Native people to victims, guides, costumes, or symbols
do not use modern slang, modern gear, vehicles, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is respectful, grounded, and useful for labor, road, and social-pressure play.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to 1899 Jefferson State western play and respects living Native communities.
